name: Lyon (France)

colors.symbolism: the silver field of the city's arms

description: A white field bearing the city's coat of arms: a red lion rampant on a silver field beneath a blue chief sown with golden fleurs-de-lis.
history: Lyon's lion arms have been the city's emblem since the medieval period; the blue chief with fleurs-de-lis was added to mark Lyon as a 'good city' of the King of France.

features.role: Coat of arms of Lyon: a red lion rampant on white with a blue chief charged with three golden fleurs-de-lis
features.symbolism: the lion is the city's medieval emblem, and the chief de France marks Lyon's status as a 'bonne ville' of the French Crown
